title: GapGuard: Two-Pointer Max Pair Sum
description: Given an array of non-negative integers and a difference limit k, find the maximum sum of any pair (a[i], a[j]) with i < j and a[j] - a[i] <= k. Use a two-pointer strategy after sorting. Return 0 if no valid pair exists. Constraints: nums length up to 2e5, 0 <= nums[i] <= 1e9, 0 <= k <= 1e9.

testCases.description: Normal case with multiple valid pairs; maximum is (7,9)
No valid pair within difference constraint
Large numbers with a valid top-end pair

hints: Sort the array and use two pointers from both ends.
Move the left pointer up when a valid pair is found; otherwise move the right pointer left.

solution.javascript: function solve(param) { const arr = (param.nums || []).slice().sort((a,b)=>a-b); const k = param.k || 0; let i = 0, j = arr.length - 1; let maxSum = 0; while (i < j) { if (arr[j] - arr[i] <= k) { maxSum = Math.max(maxSum, arr[i] + arr[j]); i++; } else { j--; } } return maxSum; }

complexity.explanation: Sort the array, then a single pass with two pointers evaluates valid pairs efficiently.
